abstract.t: This document defines an interoperability profile for implementations of the Shared Signals Framework () and the Continuous Access Evaluation Profile (). It specifies required attributes for SSF endpoints, how to use OAuth 2.0 for their authorization, and the core use cases to improve security of authenticated sessions. When implemented, the profile enables seamless interoperability between SSF Transmitters and Receivers.

section.name: Introduction
section.t: The Shared Signals Framework enables sharing of Security Event Tokens (SETs) between cooperating peers. When combined with Continuous Access Evaluation Profile () to share events such as Session Revocation and Credential Change, implementations can greatly improve their session and security outcomes.

The CAEP Interoperability Profile outlines the minimum required features that implementations must offer in order to be considered compliant and to achieve interoperability. It describes specific use cases with CAEP session-revoked, credential-change, and device-compliance-change events. Support for all use cases listed herein is not required in order to be considered compliant with this profile. An implementation can choose specific use cases to support.
The following specifications are profiled in this document:

list.t: Shared Signals Framework Specification 1.0
Continuous Access Evaluation Profile 1.0 ()
OAuth 2.0

section.name: Notational Conventions
section.t: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "NOT RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in BCP 14 when, and only when, they appear in all capitals, as shown here.

section.name: Common Requirements
section.t: The following requirements are common across all use cases defined in this document.

list.t: The SSF Transmitter MUST offer TLS protected endpoints and MUST establish connections to other servers using TLS. TLS connections MUST be set up to use TLS version 1.2 or later.
The SSF Transmitter MUST follow the recommendations for Secure Use of Transport Layer Security in .
The SSF Receiver MUST perform TLS server certificate signature checks, chain of trust validations, expiry and revocation status checks before calling the SSF Transmitter APIs, as per .

section.name: CAEP Specification Version
section.t: This specification supports CAEP events from OpenID Continuous Access Evaluation Profile 1.0.

section.name: Transmitters
section.t: Transmitters MUST implement the following features.

section.name: Specification Version
section.t: The Transmitter Configuration Metadata MUST include a spec_version field, and its value MUST be 1_0 or greater.

section.name: Delivery Method
section.t: The Transmitter Configuration Metadata MUST include the delivery_methods_supported field.

section.name: JWKS URI
section.t: The Transmitter Configuration Metadata MUST include the jwks_uri field, and upon resolution, its value MUST provide the current signing key(s) of the Transmitter.

section.name: Configuration Endpoint
section.t: The Transmitter Configuration Metadata MUST include the configuration_endpoint field as defined in Section 7.1.
The endpoint identified by configuration_endpoint is used to perform the Create Stream operation as defined in Section 8.1.1.

section.name: Status Endpoint
section.t: The Transmitter Configuration Metadata MUST include the status_endpoint field.
The endpoint identified by status_endpoint MUST support the Read Stream Status (HTTP GET) operation as defined in Section 8.1.2.1.

section.name: Verification Endpoint
section.t: The Transmitter Configuration Metadata MUST include the verification_endpoint field.
The endpoint identified by verification_endpoint MUST support Stream Verification as defined in Section 8.1.4.

section.name: Authorization Schemes
section.t: The Transmitter Configuration Metadata MUST include the authorization_schemes field and its value MUST include the value:

section.name: Streams
section.t: Transmitters MUST support all required properties and API contracts defined by Stream Management API operations, in addition to the requirements specified in this section.
For all Stream Management API requests received by the Transmitter, the following MUST be true.

section.name: Delivery
section.t: The Transmitter MUST support Create Stream requests whose delivery.method is set to one of the following values:

list.t: urn:ietf:rfc:8935 (Push)
urn:ietf:rfc:8936 (Poll)

section.t: When a Receiver makes a Create Stream request as defined in Section 8.1.1.1 with valid authorization, the Transmitter MUST process the request as specified in .
Upon successful creation of a Stream, the Transmitter MUST include a delivery field in the resulting Stream Configuration, and its method value MUST be one of the delivery methods listed above.

dd.t: The Transmitter MUST support the Create Stream operation as defined in Section 8.1.1.1 when invoked by a Receiver with valid authorization.
The Transmitter MUST support the Read Stream Configuration operation as defined in Section 8.1.1.2 when invoked by a Receiver with valid authorization.
The Transmitter MUST support the Read Stream Status operation as defined in Section 8.1.2.1 when invoked by a Receiver with valid authorization.

The Transmitter MUST support Stream Verification as defined in Section 8.1.4.2 and MUST generate a Verification Event as defined in Section 8.1.4.1 when requested by a Receiver with valid authorization.
The Transmitter MUST support the Delete Stream operation as defined in Section 8.1.1.5 when invoked by a Receiver with valid authorization.

section.name: Receivers
section.t: Receivers MUST implement the following features.

section.name: Delivery Methods
section.t: Receivers MUST be able to accept events using any one of the two delivery methods:

list.t: Push-Based Security Event Token (SET) Delivery Using HTTP
Poll-Based Security Event Token (SET) Delivery Using HTTP

section.name: JWKS URI
section.t: The Receiver MUST obtain the Transmitter's signing key(s) using the jwks_uri from the Transmitter Configuration Metadata, as defined in Section 7.1.

section.name: Authorization Schemes
section.t: The Receiver MUST use OAuth 2.0 when making Stream Management API requests to the Transmitter.

section.name: Implicitly Added Subjects
section.t: The Receiver MUST assume that all subjects are implicitly included in a Stream, without any Add Subject method invocations.

section.name: Streams
section.t: Receivers MUST support all required properties and API contracts defined by Stream Management API operations, in addition to the requirements specified in this section.
For Stream Management API requests initiated by the Receiver, and for Streams created by the Receiver, the following MUST be true.

section.name: Delivery
section.t: When creating a Stream using the Create Stream operation defined in Section 8.1.1.1, the Receiver MUST include a delivery object whose method value is one of the following, or omit the delivery object.

list.t: urn:ietf:rfc:8935 (Push)
urn:ietf:rfc:8936 (Poll)

section.t: If the Create Stream request does not include the delivery property, it is assumed to have a delivery method of urn:ietf:rfc:8936 (Poll), as defined in .

section.name: Stream Control
section.t: The following Stream Management operations MUST be supported by the Receiver.

dd.t: The Receiver MUST be able to invoke the Create Stream operation as defined in Section 8.1.1.1 using valid authorization.
The Receiver MUST be able to invoke the Read Stream Configuration operation as defined in Section 8.1.1.2 using valid authorization.
The Receiver MUST be able to invoke the Read Stream Status operation as defined in Section 8.1.2.1 using valid authorization.

The Receiver MUST be able to initiate Stream Verification as defined in Section 8.1.4.2 using valid authorization, and MUST be able to process the resulting Verification Event as defined in Section 8.1.4.1.
The Receiver MUST be able to invoke the Delete Stream operation as defined in Section 8.1.1.5 using valid authorization.

section.name: Event Subjects
section.t: The following subject identifier formats from "Subject Identifiers for Security Event Tokens" MUST be supported:

list.t: opaque (for the Verification event only)

section.t: Receivers MUST be prepared to accept events with any of the subject identifier formats specified in this section. Transmitters MUST be able to send events with at least one of the subject identifier formats specified in this section.

section.name: Event Signatures
section.t: All events MUST be signed using the RS256 algorithm with a minimum of 2048-bit keys.

section.name: OAuth Support
section.t: Implementations MUST support OAuth 2.0 . In this context, the OAuth 2.0 roles map to SSF roles as follows:

list.t: The Resource Server is the SSF Transmitter.
The Client is the SSF Receiver.
The Authorization Server is an entity trusted by the SSF Transmitter to issue access tokens.

section.name: Authorization Server
section.t: An OAuth Authorization Server issues access tokens. In the context of this profile, the Authorization Server that issues access tokens can be a different entity than the SSF Transmitter.

list.t: The Authorization Server MAY distribute discovery metadata (such as the authorization endpoint) via Authorization Server Metadata as specified in . The method in which the Receiver determines which Authorization Server to use is out of scope.

The Authorization Server MUST support at least one of the following to issue a short-lived access token to the Receiver:
client credentials grant Section 4.4 authorization code grant Section 4.1

section.t: A short-lived access token is defined as one whose lifetime is no more than 60 minutes. Please refer to access token lifetimes in the security considerations of for additional considerations.

section.name: The SSF Transmitter as a Resource Server

list.t: MUST accept access tokens in the HTTP header as in Section 2.1 of OAuth 2.0 Bearer Token Usage
MUST NOT accept access tokens via the URI query parameter mechanism described in Section 2.3 of OAuth 2.0 Bearer Token Usage
MUST verify the validity, integrity, expiration and revocation status of access tokens
MUST verify that the authorization represented by the access token is sufficient for the requested resource access
If the access token is not sufficient for the requested action, the Resource Server MUST return errors as per Section 3.1 of

section.name: OAuth Scopes
section.t: Scopes values beginning with 'ssf.' are reserved and MUST only be defined by the SSF specifications. This is to ensure there is no unintended scope name collisions.
An OAuth Authorization Server issuing tokens to SSF Receivers MUST support the following scopes:

The ssf.read scope allows Read Stream Configuration and Get Stream Status operations. The ssf.manage scope includes all ssf.read permissions and additionally allows Create Stream, Delete Stream, and Stream Verification operations.

section.name: The "events" claim
section.t: The "events" claim of the SET MUST contain only one event.

section.name: Use Cases
section.t: An implementation conforming to this profile MUST support at least one of the following use cases.

section.name: Session Revocation / Logout
section.t: In order to support session revocation or logout, implementations MUST support the CAEP event type session-revoked. The reason_admin field of the event MUST be populated with a non-empty object.

section.name: Credential Change
section.t: In order to support notifying and responding to credential changes, implementations MUST support the CAEP event type credential-change. Within the credential-change event, implementations MUST support the following field values:

dd.t: Receivers MUST interpret all allowable values of this field. Transmitters MAY generate any allowable value of this field
Receivers MUST interpret all allowable values of this field. Transmitters MAY generate any allowable value of this field
Transmitters MUST populate this value with a non-empty object

section.name: Device Compliance Change
section.t: In order to support notifying and responding to changes in device compliance status, implementations MUST support the CAEP event type device-compliance-change. This event is used to signal that a device's adherence to a set of security or organizational compliance policies has changed. Implementations MUST support the following field values:

dd.t: Receivers MUST interpret all allowable values of this field. Transmitters MAY generate any allowable value of this field
Receivers MUST interpret all allowable values of this field. Transmitters MAY generate any allowable value of this field
Transmitters MUST populate this value with a non-empty object

section.name: Security Considerations
section.t: There are no additional security considerations that arise from this document. Security considerations applicable to this profile are covered in the "Security Considerations" sections of and .

section.name: Acknowledgements
section.t: The authors wish to thank all members of the OpenID Foundation Shared Signals Working Group who contributed to the development of this specification.

section.name: Notices
section.t: Copyright (c) 2026 The OpenID Foundation.

The OpenID Foundation (OIDF) grants to any Contributor, developer, implementer, or other interested party a non-exclusive, royalty free, worldwide copyright license to reproduce, prepare derivative works from, distribute, perform and display, this Implementers Draft, Final Specification, or Final Specification Incorporating Errata Corrections solely for the purposes of (i) developing specifications, and (ii) implementing Implementers Drafts, Final Specifications, and Final Specification Incorporating Errata Corrections based on such documents, provided that attribution be made to the OIDF as the source of the material, but that such attribution does not indicate an endorsement by the OIDF.

The technology described in this specification was made available from contributions from various sources, including members of the OpenID Foundation and others. Although the OpenID Foundation has taken steps to help ensure that the technology is available for distribution, it takes no position regarding the validity or scope of any intellectual property or other rights that might be claimed to pertain to the implementation or use of the technology described in this specification or the extent to which any license under such rights might or might not be available; neither does it represent that it has made any independent effort to identify any such rights. The OpenID Foundation and the contributors to this specification make no (and hereby expressly disclaim any) warranties (express, implied, or otherwise), including implied warranties of merchantability, non-infringement, fitness for a particular purpose, or title, related to this specification, and the entire risk as to implementing this specification is assumed by the implementer. The OpenID Intellectual Property Rights policy (found at openid.net) requires contributors to offer a patent promise not to assert certain patent claims against other contributors and against implementers. OpenID invites any interested party to bring to its attention any copyrights, patents, patent applications, or other proprietary rights that may cover technology that may be required to practice this specification.

section.name: Document History
section.t: [[ To be removed from the final specification ]]

list.t: Updated OAuth section with clarifications (#325)
Minor fixes for publishing (#324)
Added Receiver requirements (#315)
Formatting fixes and device compliance change (#313)
Reworked introduction to remove normative language (#302)
Updated spec references (#291)
Clarify relationship between Transmitter and AS (#245)
Non-normative changes (#196)
Added security considerations (#190)
Added notational conventions (#190)
Updated network layer protection guidance (#213)
Updated out of date links (#227)
Updated required SSF spec version to 1_0
Added document history (#189)
Specify one event per "events" claim of SET (#179)
Include OAuth specifics (#134)
Cleaned up markdown (#91)
Add opaque to the required subject formats (#137)
Initial draft
